judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable depth; Report 2 is marginally more rigorous (blob-hash verification, explicit merge-base freshness check, test-suite and force-push considerations), but Report 1 is equally correct and complete.

report_1.evidence: `main`. Per project documentation's dead-base rule, every PR should target the trunk, not another feature branch — and in this case that's doubly true because the branch `feat/pairwise-metric` was actually stacked on (`feat/weighted-scorer`) is no longer a live, useful base at all

report_2.evidence: `feat/weighted-scorer` is not a valid target: it's the branch that was *already merged* into `main` (via a squash-merge PR #42), so its raw commits are now stale/orphaned relative to `main`'s history

report_1.evidence: That starting point is now dead: `feat/weighted-scorer`'s entire content was already merged into `main` via a **different**, squashed commit — `1971f08` ("Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)", 2026-06-13)... `git merge-base --is-ancestor ac9d505 1971f08` → `no`

report_2.evidence: has since been **merged into `main`, but via a squash merge** (PR #42, commit `1971f08`, dated 2026-06-13, message "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)")... `git merge-base main feat/pairwise-metric` is still the pre-divergence `65204ad`, not `1971f08`

report_1.evidence: it also reincludes the entire W1–W3 weighted-scorer diff to `scoring/scorer.py` and part of `scoring/metrics.py`... This extra content is present purely because of the squash-merge history divergence explained in Q2 — it is not part of the pairwise-comparison feature

report_2.evidence: It additionally includes the entire `feat/weighted-scorer` change set — the `overlap()` helper, the `WEIGHTS` dict, and the `scorer.py` rewrite... This isn't an accidental scope-creep by the author — it's a structural artifact of branching off a feature branch that later got squash-merged elsewhere.

report_1.evidence: Re-verify after rebasing: confirm `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` (three-dot) now matches the tip-to-tip diff (2 files, ~5 lines) with zero unrelated `scorer.py`/W-series content, and confirm `git merge-tree $(git merge-base main feat/pairwise-metric) main feat/pairwise-metric` reports a clean merge with no conflict markers.

report_2.evidence: After rebasing, re-verify: `git diff main feat/pairwise-metric` (two-dot) and `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` (three-dot, merge-base) should now be identical and show only the 2-file / 5-line pairwise-metric change — confirming the merge-base has moved to `1971f08` and no weighted-scorer content is being re-proposed.
